_note: Source for build_data.py. Each place is geocoded from `address`, or uses explicit `lat`/`lng` when the location is only approximately known (private estates, unbuilt developments). `approximate:true` is surfaced in the UI.

places.name: Blue Heron Farm
places.agents: Maggie Gold Seelig (MGS Group) & Brian Dougherty (Corcoran)
places.blurb: The island's record sale: a 30-acre waterfront compound famous as the Obamas' summer rental, bought by billionaire Les Wexner in summer 2025. Architect Norman Foster bought it for $22.4M in 2011 and renovated it into a main house, guest house, boat house, tennis court, riding paddocks and private beach.

places.name: The Obama Estate
places.blurb: After years of renting on the island, the Obamas bought a 29-acre waterfront estate fronting Edgartown Great Pond in 2019.

places.name: Chip Chop
places.blurb: The longtime Vineyard Haven estate of journalist Diane Sawyer, bought by prolific island investor David Malm for $23.9M in 2023.

places.name: Faraway Edgartown (Kelley House)
places.blurb: Blue Flag Capital acquired an assemblage in downtown Edgartown including the historic Kelley House for $19.6M in 2020, redeveloping it into the second New England outpost of its Faraway hotel brand.

places.name: Black Dog Tavern
places.address: 20 Beach Street Extension, Vineyard Haven, MA 02568
places.blurb: The famed Vineyard Haven waterfront restaurant, whose black-dog-with-a-red-collar merch spawned a retail empire stretching from Maine to Georgia.

places.name: Coastwise Pub
places.blurb: A new restaurant from the Black Dog's Dan Pucillo, opening in Oak Bluffs this summer.

places.name: Green Villa
places.blurb: The island's largest housing project ever: a 116-unit development from William Cumming's Atwood Company, using Massachusetts' 40B program. It sets aside 12 units for affordable buyers and 30 for island businesses to buy for employees.
